FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE - April 10, 2026
Vancouver-based tech firm SkyLine Systems Awarded $1.5M Government Contract
VANCOUVER, BC — The Ministry of Transport has selected SkyLine Systems to develop a next-generation traffic monitoring solution for the Lower Mainland.
The 18-month project will require a dedicated team of 12 engineers and analysts. 
Work is scheduled to begin in June 2026 at their main office located in Yaletown.
"This is a significant milestone for SkyLine as we expand our footprint in smart city infrastructure," said Jane Doe, CEO.
The project encompasses the deployment of smart sensors across major arterial routes in Burnaby and Richmond.
Total duration: 540 days.
Approximate start date: 2026-06-15.
Contact: media@skylinesystems.ca
